questions.text: A change in which only physical properties of a substance change is a:
questions.explanation: Physical changes involve changes in shape, size, or state, but no new substance is formed.

questions.text: Which of the following is a chemical change?
questions.explanation: Rusting produces a new substance (iron oxide), making it a chemical change.

questions.text: The process of depositing a layer of zinc on iron is called:
questions.explanation: Galvanization is used to prevent iron from rusting.

questions.text: Which gas is evolved when baking soda reacts with vinegar?
questions.explanation: The reaction produces carbon dioxide gas, which turns lime water milky.

questions.text: Crystallization is an example of a:
questions.explanation: Large crystals of pure substances are formed from their solutions without creating new substances.

questions.text: What color is Copper Sulphate solution?
questions.explanation: Copper Sulphate (Blue vitriol) forms a characteristic blue solution in water.

questions.text: When magnesium ribbon burns in air, it produces:
questions.explanation: Burning magnesium produces magnesium oxide, which appears as a powdery white ash.

questions.text: An iron nail dipped in copper sulphate solution turns brownish due to the deposition of:
questions.explanation: This is a displacement reaction where iron displaces copper from the solution.

questions.text: Which of these is necessary for rusting to occur?
questions.explanation: Iron rusts only when it comes into contact with both air (oxygen) and moisture (water).

questions.text: A chemical change is also called a:
questions.explanation: A change in which one or more new substances are formed is called a chemical reaction.
